Resolves #14574

The design-partner entry lands: `connectTenant({tenantUrl, credential})` — point the cockpit at a hosted Agent-OS tenant instead of standing up the local stack. `FleetTenantService` (new, sibling-patterned on `FleetRegistryService`'s store discipline) validates the URL (http/https only, credential-embedded URLs rejected outright), authenticates the transport through an injected bearer probe (`/health` + `Authorization: Bearer`, bounded timeout; 401/403 = named auth rejection), persists the public descriptor (`tenants.json`) plus the PAT in a separate encrypted store (`tenant-credentials.enc`, AES-256-GCM, `0600`, the same `NEO_FLEET_SECRET_KEY`/keyfile source as the agent-PAT store — one operator secret governs both reversible credential classes), and records `deploymentClass: 'cloud-tenant'` (the #14456 posture marker). The return is the public descriptor or a controlled `{status: 'rejected', reason}` — never the PAT, never a raw transport error.

Wire surface: `connectTenant` + `listTenants` on `FleetControlBridge` (injectable `tenantService` seam, the `defineAgent` secret-omission boundary) and on `FLEET_WIRE_METHODS`; the browser bridge generates both automatically. The Brain-internal `getCredential` reader is deliberately OFF the wire — pinned by a dispatch-rejection spec.

Evidence: L3 achieved (the full fleet unit suite green incl. the new security matrix; the generated-bridge exact-equality spec auto-covers the new wire methods) → L3 required (the close-target ACs are service/wire-contract claims, mechanically provable). Residual: the remote-AGENT-roster mirror (below).

## Deltas from ticket

- AC-3 ("remote roster/status render through the same cockpit read path") is delivered at the TENANT grain: `listTenants` rides the same read-observe wire class as `fleetRoster`, so the cockpit renders connected tenants (endpoint/status/posture) exactly like local fleet views. Mirroring the remote tenant's own AGENT roster (querying the hosted MC over the authenticated transport) is a distinct consumer of the credential this leaf stores — reconciled on the ticket as the named follow-up rather than smuggled in here; the `getCredential` Brain-internal seam is its ready entry point.
- Reconnecting an existing endpoint updates descriptor + credential in place (re-auth is the point of a reconnect) — one endpoint, one tenant id (host + endpoint digest).

## Test Evidence

- `npx playwright test --config=test/playwright/playwright.config.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/services/fleet` — **173 passed** (9 new: the connect security matrix — public-descriptor shape, **PAT-never-echoed deep-scan** across the result, `listTenants`, the public store file, AND the encrypted file's plaintext; fail-closed URL validation incl. credential-embedded URLs; bounded rejection reasons that cannot leak a throwing transport's dump; reconnect-in-place; bridge delegation; wire allowlist + the `getCredential` dispatch-rejection negative; the fail-closed wire envelope).
- `createFleetRegistryBridge.spec` asserts the generated browser bridge's keys equal `FLEET_WIRE_METHODS` exactly — the AC's client-bridge exposure proof, auto-covering both new methods.
- Directly touched surfaces: `ai/services/fleet/` + `src/ai/fleet/fleetWireMethods.mjs` + specs; no app/UI surface (the connect pane is #14560's leaf).

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] A live connect against a hosted tenant (operator-held PAT) — the probe path against real infrastructure.
- [ ] The remote-agent-roster follow-up consumes `getCredential` for the authenticated transport.

## Review routing note

Cross-family primary on CI-green per the family rule. Security-sensitive surface (credential store) — reviewer attention on the PAT-boundary specs is the highest-value pass.

Related: #13015 (parent) · #14560 (the connect UI leaf) · #14456 (deploymentClass posture) · #14537 (blocked sibling verb).
